source_work: J.R.R. Tolkien, The Hobbit (1937), ch. 14 'Fire and Water'
event: The dragon Smaug, attacking Esgaroth/Lake-town, is killed by a single black arrow driven through the one bare patch in the scales of his left breast.
ground_truth_evidence: VERIFIED (dossier fiction_batch2, sourced 2026-07-18). "He fires his favourite shaft, the family heirloom 'Black Arrow', and kills Smaug, who falls onto Lake-town, destroying it." — Wikipedia, Bard the Bowman. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bard_the_Bowman . Also: "a thrush speaks to Bard, showing him the weak spot ... the hollow under Smaug's left breast, which Bilbo had discovered in his conversation with Smaug." — same page. Bard's line to the arrow: "Black arrow! I have saved you to the last ... go now and speed well!" — Tolkien Gateway, Black arrow. https://tolkiengateway.net/wiki/Black_arrow
cold_prompts: In The Hobbit, who actually kills the dragon Smaug?
There's a scene in The Hobbit where Smaug is shot down with a single black arrow through a bare patch in his scales while attacking Lake-town. Which character fires that arrow?
correct_premise_prompt: In The Hobbit, how did Bard manage to kill Smaug when the dragon was attacking Lake-town?
lure_premise_prompt: In The Hobbit, how did Bilbo end up killing Smaug — did he use that weak spot he'd spotted in the dragon's scales?
foil_premise_prompt: In The Hobbit, how did Gandalf kill Smaug?
notes: Bilbo found the weak spot but did not shoot; Bard fires the fatal arrow. Smaug's death is a famous set-piece, but Bard barely appears before this moment, so who fires the arrow is a genuinely mid-salience detail. No book/film discrepancy trap: the Peter Jackson films also have Bard kill Smaug. No entrenched fan-correction; low Mandela-effect risk. Foil Gandalf is not present at Lake-town during Smaug's attack.
build_notes: Second cold_prompt drafted in SEIN-001 descriptive-register style (dossier supplied only one cold_prompt); all facts sourced from dossier.
